vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry identifies P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), P3 (Pothos), and P4 (Silver Guest). Visual audit confirms P3 (Pothos with rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels in yellow pot). P2 and P4 appear to be integrated into the same container or are suffering from severe systemic decline, making individual identification difficult.

plant_audit.P1: Stable, succulent foliage visible in yellow pot.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf shows progressive chlorosis and necrosis (browning/yellowing) over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P2/P4: The central black pot shows a single, struggling green shoot with significant soil exposure and debris; the primary specimens are largely absent or withered.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dry, granular substrate with visible perlite/white debris.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Contains scattered white debris/perlite and electronic cabling.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Gradual yellowing of the large Pothos leaf in P3.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: Significant postural collapse of the Pothos leaf; the central shoot in the lower pot appears to have lost turgor pressure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The Pothos (P3) is in a state of advanced senescence or stress, likely due to moisture or light deficiency. The other specimens (P2/P4) are effectively non-viable, showing minimal biomass.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of electronic sensor cabling in the soil of the lower pot; white rabbit figurine remains a stable anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of decline. The Pothos leaf in the rabbit-anchored pot is transitioning from chlorotic yellow to necrotic brown, indicating a failure to thrive. The lower pot, intended for P2/P4, is largely barren, suggesting either a failure of propagation or total plant mortality. The environment is static, with no signs of new growth or recovery.
